edge_cases.reason: The token is a valid merchant name but includes trailing punctuation (a period). The current validator treats the punctuated token as invalid instead of normalising it. After stripping the trailing period the token matches a known merchant (Vons).
edge_cases.suggested_rule: When a token matches a known merchant (or passes fuzzy matching) after stripping trailing punctuation (.,;:), treat it as MERCHANT_NAME rather than rejecting it.

edge_cases.reason: The token is a generic business‑entity suffix, not the full trading name. In receipts it appears as part of a longer name (e.g., "JPMorgan Chase Bank, N.A.") and should not be labelled as MERCHANT_NAME on its own.
edge_cases.suggested_rule: Exclude isolated tokens that are common business suffixes (Bank, Inc, LLC, Co, Ltd, Corp, N.A., etc.) when they appear alone or only with trailing punctuation; they should not be assigned MERCHANT_NAME.
